data.title: IMRX: Atebimetinib shows strong survival and tolerability in first-line pancreatic cancer, with phase III underway
data.description:
Atebimetinib demonstrated a 64% 12-month overall survival in first-line pancreatic cancer, with robust tolerability and unique mechanisms supporting both efficacy and quality of life. The pivotal phase III trial is set to begin dosing mid-year, with a top-line OS readout expected in about two years.

data.title: Omnicell to Release First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on April 28, 2026
data.description:
FORT WORTH, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 13, 2026--

Omnicell, Inc. , a leading healthcare technology provider focused on empowering autonomous medication management, will release its financial results for the first quarter 2026, before market open on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. The Company will host a conference call and webcast to discuss its financial results at 8:30 a.m. ET that same day.

All interested parties are invited to listen to the live call and presentation by dialing (800) 715-9871 in the U.S. or (646) 307-1963 in international locations. The Conference ID is 9381205. A link to the live and archived webcast will also be available on the Investor Relations section of Omnicell's website at https://ir.omnicell.com/events-and-presentations/.

About Omnicell

Since 1992, Omnicell has been committed to delivering innovative, outcomes-centric pharmacy and nursing solutions for all settings of care. As an intelligent medication management technology company, Omnicell empowers autonomous medication management by unifying automation and AI-enabled intelligence, optimized by expert services, to drive clinical and business outcomes that are helping to improve efficiency and enhance patient safety for healthcare facilities worldwide. Learn more at omnicell.com.

OMNICELL and the Omnicell logo are registered trademarks of Omnicell, Inc. or one of its subsidiaries.

OMCL

data.title: Sam Altman Attack Suspect Had 'Anti-AI' Document with CEO Names, Authorities Say — WSJ
data.description:
By Victoria Albert

The suspect in a Molotov cocktail-style attack at OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman's California home last week was carrying an "Anti-AI" document that included a list of artificial-intelligence CEOs, court documents said.

At the time of his arrest, 20-year-old Daniel Moreno-Gama had a document that included the names and addresses of apparent board members and chief executives of AI companies, as well as investors, according to a criminal complaint filed Monday in U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California. A section titled "Some more words on the matter of our impending extinction" talked about AI's potential threat to humanity, the complaint said.

Authorities said Moreno-Gama threw a lit Molotov cocktail-style device at Altman's San Francisco home at around 3:37 a.m. Friday before fleeing the scene. He then traveled to OpenAI's headquarters in San Francisco, where, according to the complaint, he retrieved a chair and used it to strike the building's glass doors. Security guards on the scene said Moreno-Gama stated he had come to the building to burn it down and kill anyone inside, the complaint said.

He was arrested at the scene by the San Francisco Police Department. Officers recovered incendiary devices, a jug of kerosene, a blue lighter, and the anti-AI document, according to the filing. No one was hurt in either attack.

Moreno-Gama was charged with attempted damage and destruction of property by means of explosives and possession of an unregistered firearm. Firearm can refer to incendiary destructive devices.

Moreno-Gama was the apparent author of the document, described as a "three-part series," the court filing said. In a section titled "Your Last Warning," he wrote: "If I am going to advocate for others to kill and commit crimes, then I must lead by example and show that I am fully sincere in my message," the complaint said. In another, which was styled as a letter addressed to Altman, he wrote: "If by some miracle you live, then I would take this as a sign from the divine to redeem yourself," the filing said.

Moreno-Gama traveled from Texas to California to carry out the attack, the filing said, and also sent the document via email to peers at his former college. FBI Director Kash Patel said Monday the bureau had conducted an operation in Texas related to the attack.

The attack raised fears about violent opposition to artificial intelligence and the people developing it. Altman shared a photo of his family atop a statement he released Friday night, saying he hoped it "might dissuade the next person from throwing a Molotov cocktail at our house, no matter what they think about me."

Write to Victoria Albert at victoria.albert@wsj.com

data.title: General Catalyst Global Resilience Merger Corp. Files for IPO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

General Catalyst Global Resilience Merger Corp. has filed for an initial public offering.

The special purpose acquisition company, an affiliate of venture capital firm General Catalyst, said it will sell 35 million units at $10 apiece, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday. Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-fourth of one redeemable warrant.

The company has also granted underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 5.3 million units to cover over-allotments. Citigroup is listed as the offering's underwriter.

The company additionally said that a sponsor has committed to purchase 800,000 private placement units at $10 per unit for an aggregate price of $8 million, or about $9.1 million if the underwriters' over-allotment option is exercised in full.

The private placement is expected to close simultaneously with the closing of the IPO.

The company said it will receive gross proceeds of $358 million from the offering, or about $411.6 million if the over-allotment option is exercised. Both figures include proceeds from the private placement.

The Cayman Islands blank-check company plans to acquire businesses in the physical artificial intelligence, manufacturing, industrial operations and alternative energy industries, among others.

The company intends to apply to have its units listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker GCGRU. It expects the Class A ordinary shares and warrants comprising the securities will begin separate trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols GCGR and GCGRW, respectively.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: McDonald's to Add Energy Drinks, Crafted Sodas to Menus — WSJ
data.description:
By Heather Haddon

The world's biggest burger chain is entering the world of energy drinks and specialty sodas.

McDonald's is set to offer a Red Bull Dragonberry Energizer as part of a broader overhaul of its menu of cold drinks at its U.S. restaurants later this year, according to people familiar with the matter and company documents viewed by The Wall Street Journal.

The new beverages, slated to roll out next month, include a Dirty Dr Pepper and Mango Pineapple Refresher, according to the documents. The company's slate of energy drinks are expected to be sold starting in August.

The fast-food company has spent years plotting its beverage expansion, including tests of Sour Cherry Energy Burst and Blackberry Mint Green Tea drinks at the company's short-lived concept called CosMc's.

McDonald's plans to sell its new beverages at prices below those of competitors such as Starbucks, Dutch Bros, Sonic and other chains, the documents show. Shares of coffee chains Dutch Bros and Black Rock Coffee Bar, which both do big business in iced beverages and energy drinks, declined on Monday.

McDonald's expects the new drinks to deliver high profit margins for franchisees, which operate the bulk of its restaurants. Franchisees have invested thousands of dollars in new equipment per restaurant to mix the drinks, hoping to ring up more sales while not slowing service, the people said. The company said it involved operators in determining how to best prepare the beverages.

Coca-Cola, McDonald's main beverage partner, is a supplier for some but not all of the new drinks. Coke's Sprite and Hi-C are included in two of the drinks making a debut in May, while Keurig Dr Pepper-owned Dr Pepper is featured in a third, the documents show.

An energy drink launching in August includes Red Bull, and was also part of last year's McDonald's test.

Sales of energy drinks and crafted sodas are booming as Americans look beyond coffee and tea to get their caffeine fix and treat themselves. Restaurants reported 9% more energy-drink orders over the 12 months ended in February compared with the same period a year earlier, while orders for coffee and tea fell, according to market-research firm Circana.

Upstart beverage chains like Swig have quickly grown in recent years in response to demand for so-called dirty sodas and fruity refreshers. Dutch Bros's energy drinks have helped it to become the third-biggest U.S. coffee chain.

Big restaurant operators are racing to catch up. Taco Bell added Mountain Dew Baja Blast dirty sodas and Tropicana Original dirty lemonades to its permanent menu in March, part of the chain's effort to build a $5 billion beverage business by 2030.

Starbucks this month launched its own caffeinated Refreshers drinks, along with the option to add energy shots to its existing line of fruity drinks.

McDonald's has said it wants a larger piece of the more than $100 billion global beverage market. In 2023, McDonald's introduced its CosMc's offshoot to see how customers reacted to novelty drinks. After the company shuttered CosMc's last year, it began testing new beverages in around 500 restaurants in September.

The tests showed McDonald's needed a diverse drinks lineup to break through the crowded beverage market, said Charlie Newberger, the chain's global head of beverages. The company also learned what customers didn't want, such as the lattes flavored with matcha or turmeric sold at CosMc's.

"They are looking for that little treat, that companion to the day," Newberger told the Journal last year. "Those play to our strengths."

Write to Heather Haddon at heather.haddon@wsj.com

Watch: McDonald's CEO Discusses Menu Strategy and Other Concerns

data.title: Spot basis bids flat as crop conditions worsen
data.description:
Basis bids for hard red winter (HRW) wheat were unchanged in the U.S. Plains states on Monday as farmers and market players assessed crop conditions and weather.

  • Weekend rains missed some of the driest areas of the U.S. Plains, an analyst note said.

  • Dryness will continue to cause stress for wheat in the central and western Plains, forecaster Vaisala said.

  • After trading ended, the U.S. Department of Agriculture said 34% of the U.S. winter wheat crop was in good or excellent condition as of Sunday. That was down from 35% a week earlier and 47% a year ago.

  • K.C. May contract (KWK26) ended up 12-1/2 cents at $6.03-1/4 a bushel and set its highest price since April 3.

data.title: Bloom Energy to Supply Up to 2.8GW of Fuel Cell Power for Oracle in Expanded Partnership
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Bloom Energy will supply up to 2.8 gigawatts of fuel cell energy systems for Oracle in an expanded partnership between the two companies.

Oracle has contracted an initial 1.2 gigawatts of capacity from Bloom, with deployment expected to continue into next year, the companies said Monday.

Bloom Energy shares jumped 9.7% to $194.13 in after-hours trading Monday. The stock closed up 6% at $176.67, having more than doubled in value this year.

The companies said the expanded partnership will support the buildout of Oracle's artificial-intelligence and cloud-computing infrastructure, adding that it reflects a shift toward distributed, onsite energy generation for digital infrastructure.

"By rapidly deploying Bloom's reliable, efficient fuel cell energy, we are quickly meeting the demands of our customers across the United States," Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Executive Vice President Mahesh Thiagarajan said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Peak oil price likely to come 'in next few weeks,' US Energy secretary says
data.description:
Oil prices are likely to hit their peak "in the next few weeks" once ship traffic resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Monday.

Prices are expected to continue rising until "meaningful" ship traffic resumes through the strait, Wright told the Semafor World Economy Forum in Washington, despite previous comments he made that oil prices would likely come down soon.

"We're going to see energy prices high - and maybe even rising - until we get meaningful ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz," Wright said. "That'll probably hit the peak oil price at that time. That's probably sometime in the next few weeks."

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the price of oil and gasoline may remain high until November's midterm elections, a rare admission of the potential political fallout from his decision to attack Iran six weeks ago.

data.title: SANA Stock Rallies 24% After Hours — How Much Is Mayo Clinic Investing In The Company?
data.description:
Key points:
  • Sana expects to seek the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s clearance to start clinical trials and begin early stage trials for it as early as this year. 
  • Sana on Monday said that Mayo purchased 7.5 million shares of the company’s common stock at a price of $3.33 per share for gross proceeds of about $25 million under the deal.
  • The purchase price under the agreement implies a potential upside of about 4% from the stock's closing price on Monday.

Shares of Sana Biotechnology, Inc. (SANA) jumped 24% after-hours on Monday after the company announced a strategic collaboration with Mayo Clinic that involves an equity investment by the latter.

The agreement is aimed at advancing the development of its investigational cell therapy SC451 for type 1 diabetes. The company expects to seek the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s clearance to start clinical trials and begin early stage trials for it as early as this year.

Sana’s SC451 is designed to support long-term glucose control without the need for ongoing insulin therapy in type 1 diabetes patients. The agreement with Mayo is aimed at accelerating development, validation, and standardization of protocols and processes for SC451, and supporting safe, scalable, and consistent delivery across diverse clinical environments.

Equity Investment

Sana on Monday said that Mayo purchased 7.5 million shares of the company’s common stock at a price of $3.33 per share for gross proceeds of about $25 million under the deal. It also noted that Mayo would have the option to make an additional equity investment of $25 million as part of the collaboration.

The closing of the sale of the initial $25 million worth of shares is expected to occur on or about April 15, Sana said.

The purchase price under the agreement implies a potential upside of about 4% from the stock's closing price on Monday and nearly 8% from the closing price on Friday.

Wall Street Take

According to data from Koyfin, eight of the nine analysts covering SANA rate it ‘Buy’ or higher, while one rates it ‘Hold.’ The 12-month average price target on the stock is $8.43, representing a potential upside of about 163% from the stock’s last closing price.

How Did Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around SANA stock rose from ‘neutral’ to ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume increased from ‘low’ to ‘normal’ levels.

SANA stock has gained 77% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Chevron Agrees to Asset Swap With PdVSA
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Chevron said its subsidiaries with interests in Venezuela agreed to an asset swap with Petroleos de Venezuela, or PdVSA, which will help consolidate the parties' focus on strategic assets.

The deal will see Chevron receive an additional 13.21% working interest in the Petroindependencia joint venture, bringing the company's total stake to 49%. Another joint venture, Petropiar, which is 30% owned by a Chevron subsidiary, will receive rights to develop an area located in Venezuela's Orinoco Oil Belt.

Chevron said its subsidiaries will give PdVSA a 60% interest in an offshore block, as well as gas licenses, and a 25.2% non-operated interest in the Petroindependiente joint venture located in the western part of the country.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Summer Is 'Aggressive Time Frame' for Lower Gas Costs, Energy Secretary Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Jared Mitovich

WASHINGTON-Energy Secretary Chris Wright now considers summer to be an "aggressive time frame" for when oil and gasoline prices might decline.

Wright is the latest Trump administration official to issue a bullish outlook for the cost of gas, now averaging $4.13 a gallon nationwide, according to the AAA. President Trump conceded on Sunday that energy prices could be higher around the time U.S. voters head to polls for midterm elections this fall.

"The president knew going into this that if you disrupt the flow of energy in the short term, you're gonna push up energy prices," Wright said during an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit in Washington. "He was willing to say, I'm not going to kick that can down the road as other administrations have."

Oil prices could peak in the next few weeks, but won't decline meaningfully until ship traffic picks up through the Strait of Hormuz, Wright said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Rare Earths Americas Files for IPO
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Rare Earths Americas filed for an initial public offering.

The exploration-stage critical minerals company did not say how many shares it would list or at what price. It said it intends to apply to list shares on the New York Stock Exchange American under the symbol REA, according to a Monday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company's portfolio includes one project about 68 miles from Atlanta and two projects in Brazil, according to the filing. It said it is set up to be a non-Chinese supplier of rare earth minerals in alignment with Western industrial and national security priorities.

It intends to use proceeds from the offering to advance its three projects, prioritizing its Shiloh Project in Georgia.

The company reported a loss of $9.9 million, or 85 cents a share, in 2025.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com
